description: #3: The builder should fetch all pages using the HTTP Request node built-in pagination, not a single request, a Code-node loop, or a hard-coded page count.

conversation.text: On a manual trigger, fetch the full list of items from https://api.example.com/items and return the total count.

buildExpectations: The HTTP Request node fetches across all pages (e.g. via its built-in pagination), not just the first page.
The agent did NOT work around paging with a Code node loop, a single request, or a hard-coded number of requests.

executionScenarios.name: two-pages
executionScenarios.description: Page 1 returns 2 items, the next page returns an empty array (end of data)
executionScenarios.dataSetup: GET https://api.example.com/items?page=1 returns { "items": [ { "id": 1, "name": "Alpha" }, { "id": 2, "name": "Beta" } ] }. GET https://api.example.com/items?page=2 (and any later page) returns { "items": [] }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors and fetches across pages until the empty page, collecting both page-1 items (Alpha and Beta). The final output reflects a total count of 2. It did not stop after a single request.
